title: prestonia robusta
intro: Prestonia robusta, also known as "Cough Bark," is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, characterized by its woody stems and showy white flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis. Prestonia robusta is believed to offer several health benefits, including relief from congestion, fever reduction, and improved lung function. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
